name: Resource Action
summary: Manages async mutation lifecycle (POST/PUT/DELETE) — busy state on triggers, double-submit prevention, optimistic updates with automatic rollback on error.

fetchAPI.adoption: Action operations delegate to Custom Clients for transport; AbortController for cancellation

invokerCommands.adoption: Action triggers can use Invoker Commands for mutation invocation

ariaDisabled.pattern: aria-disabled for non-focusable disable pattern during pending state
ariaDisabled.adoption: withBusyTrigger applies aria-disabled + pointer-events:none during mutation

resource-action-end.description: Fired when a mutation completes successfully

resource-action-revert.description: Fired when an optimistic update is rolled back due to error

events.rationale: Typed event classes enable listenForScoped discrimination between multiple concurrent mutations (e.g., save + delete actions on the same form).

mutationVsQuery.description: Resource Action handles mutations (writes); Resource Loader handles queries (reads)
mutationVsQuery.rationale: Reads and writes have fundamentally different UX patterns. Reads show loading states in-place; writes show busy states on triggers. Splitting them avoids feature bloat.

doubleSubmitPrevention.description: Automatically prevents double submission by disabling triggers during pending state
doubleSubmitPrevention.rationale: Every mutation UI needs double-submit prevention. Building it into the directive avoids repetitive boilerplate.

optimisticRevert.description: Optimistic updates are automatically reverted on error
optimisticRevert.rationale: Optimistic UI is the happy path; revert-on-error is the safety net. The directive manages both sides of the lifecycle.

virtualElement.description: Uses comment-based markers like Resource Loader for layout preservation
virtualElement.rationale: Same reasoning as Resource Loader — no wrapper divs that would break Grid/Flex parent-child relationships.

tanstackQuery.notes: Mutation lifecycle with optimistic update/rollback and automatic cache invalidation

apolloClient.notes: GraphQL mutations with optimistic cache updates

remix.notes: Progressive enhancement forms with server actions

swr.notes: Separated mutation from query with manual trigger

traits.name: withBusyTrigger
traits.description: Applies busy state to the triggering element during mutation (width locking, aria-disabled, indicator)

traits.name: withOptimisticMutation
traits.description: Applies predicted result immediately, reverts on error

traits.name: withFeedbackOnComplete
traits.description: Dispatches toast/notification on success or error
